# Prerequisites

<p style="font-size: 20px;"><span style="color: #cf6679;">This Service is using Aleph initialization. Please refer to Aleph documentation</span></p>

[Link to Aleph Documentation](https://dev.azure.com/mit-us/PrimeWiki/_wiki/wikis/PrimeWiki.wiki/28879/Special-ENV-Variables-in-Prime)

As well you are welcome to watch the 2023 MPE-U presentation video to see the bigger picture of PUP architecture

https://videoportal.intel.com/media/PUP+%28Per+Unit+Plist%29+%E2%80%93++AI+driven+test+cost+reduction+solution.-20231018/0_36ad5i60

# PupService
The PupServiceis part of the PUP (Per Unit Plist) solution, which its goal is to perform a TTR (Test Time Reduction) evaluating every instance which executes a plist, in order to determine if it should execute a **reduced plist version**.
It is relevant for sockets where InfraBLL operates - for example CLASS socket. On SOFT for example where InfraBLL does not operate at the moment  -  PUP is implemented differrently (described below)

PUP is a methodology which operates on VminSearch instances and their extentions. 
PupServices performs required actions as parsing [configuration files](#Configuration-Files), create [reduced plist verisons](#Creating-Slim-patlist-version-mechanism), and provide the mechanism to implement the [pattern to execute's trigger](#Patlist-execution).

# Configuration Files
PupService works with two configuration files. The first one is the [pattern file](#Pattern-file), which indicates configuration related to patlist, and the second one is the [pup file](#PUP-file), which indicates conditions and settings to use the new patlist defined by the first configuration file.

It's important to mention that both of them are **json** format, and they are required in order to use the current service.

## Pattern file
The pattern configuration file specifies a per plist list of patterns that will be excluded from the copy generated from that plist (**slim patlist version**). The slim plist copies are generated during Init phase - specifically during the verify of PrimePupTestMethod. This file is generated by the product TTR team and distributed to development sites using TP torrent's CTTR/PUP panel.

Patterns file path should be defined under PUP_PATTERNS_DIR token in Env file , specifying the "dynamic" path using **@LATEST_REV@** token
Example: PUP_PATTERNS_DIR = "./Modules/PUP/InputFiles/@LATEST_REV@/patterns.pup.json";
@LATEST_REV@ - indicates dynamically resolved file path. the actual path part is resolved from latest.txt file residing in the same directory where written the actual path part

![image.png](./.attachments/image-e90ee7cc-72ef-4282-98b4-e24aeb8d6660.png).

According to the below example the resolved path is: I:\ulat\PUP\ADLXXXX\1007\patterns.pup.json

In case the plist contains pattern duplications -  using "Occurrence" field user can specify which plist occurrence to exclude from slim version. This field accepts a range of values - example: "1,2,5-7". If no occurrences are indicated, the number of the occurrence **one** (_1_) will be taken as default.

This file is parsed using [Aleph mechanism](https://dev.azure.com/mit-us/PrimeWiki/_wiki/wikis/PrimeWiki.wiki/28879/Special-ENV-Variables-in-Prime), and this means that the file name should match the following regex `.*pup.json`, for example `pattern.pup.json`.

Patterns file has a "Version" field to differentiate between the files using version numbers.
Similar to PUP file, patterns file contents specified per __*process step*__ and __*process type*__.

As follows an example of the content of this file:
```json
{
	"Version": "1234",
	"ProcessTypes": [
        {
            "Name": "CLASSHOT",
            "StepName": "CLASSHOT",
            "PerPatlistPatternsToDisable": [{
                    "Patlist": "incremental_sub_plist_2",
                    "PatternsToDisable": [{
                            "Pattern": "g3213851V0511530B"
                        },
                        {
                            "Pattern": "incremental2",
                            "Occurrence": "1,2"
                        }
                    ]
                },
                {
                    "Patlist": "incremental_search_plist",
                    "PatternsToDisable": [{
                        "Pattern": "g3213851V0511530B"
                    }]
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "Name": "CLASSCOLD",
            "StepName": "CLASSCOLD",
            "PerPatlistPatternsToDisable": [
                {
                    "Patlist": "incremental_sub_plist_2",
                    "PatternsToDisable": [{
                            "Pattern": "g3213851V0511530B"
                        },
                        {
                            "Pattern": "incremental2",
                            "Occurrence": "1,2"
                        }
                    ]
                }
            ]
        }
    ]
}
```

Field details:
- _**Version**_:  patterns file version number (optional)
- _**PerPatlistPatternsToDisable**_: Under this key or label all the slim versions will be defined. The label's value is a list of reduced or slim patlist specifications. Every patlist specification contains the `Patlist` and `PatternsToDisable` field.
  - _**ProcessTypes**_: patterns data is specified per process type and process step pair
    - _**Name**_: type name should match the SCVars.SC_CURRENT_PROCESS_TYPE user var
    - _**StepName**_: step name should match the SCVars.SC_CURRENT_PROCESS_STEP user var
      - _**PerPatlistPatternsToDisable**_: Under this key or label all the slim versions will be defined. The label's value is a list of reduced or slim patlist specifications. Every patlist specification contains the `Patlist` and `PatternsToDisable` field.
        - _**Patlist**_: Field to provide corresponding full patlist's name, which is the base to generate the reduced patlist version.
        - _**PatternsToDisable**_: Field to provide the corresponding patterns to disable from the full patlist version in order to generate the reduced one. The field's value is a list of patterns specifications, which contains the `pattern` name and the `occurrences`.
          - _**Pattern**_: Field to provide the corresponding pattern name to disable.
          - _**Occurrence**_: Optional field with default value **1**, to provide the corresponding occurrence to disable.

## PUP file
The PUP configuration file is generated by the station controller during lot introduction using a script provided by InfraBLL team. The file location is specified by the script in **SCVars.SC_PUP_FILE_PATH** uservar.

Prime reads this file during the verify phase of PrimePupTestMethod instance.
PUP file has two main sections - condifsets definition and per unit action definitions.
ConfigSet is an entity that connects between plist and instances names where this plist is executed.

Unit info contains the die IDs as VID and Efuse and the action to perform in specific configSet(s).
Action type are those:
1. FULL - Instances defined in configSet will execute full/original plist version
2. SHORT - Instances defined in configSet will execute slim/ reduced plist version
3. MONITOR - Instances defined in configSet will execute the full version while executing the inline RV (TTR monitor algorithm)

PUP file name should match the following regex `.*pup.json`. Usually, the file name will consist of the lot name and extension - for example `Y1234567MV.pup.json`.

```json
{
	"MaestroTpName":"ADL0000D00S135,ADL0000E00S137",
	"PatternsFilePath": "~HDMT_TPL_DIR/Modules/PUP/PUP/InputFiles/BOM/1002/patterns.pup.json",
	"PupConfigSets": [
	{
		"Name":"CHPUP1A1BPLIST",
			"Information": [
			{
				"Patlist":"scn_grt_x_vccia_f3_chkf3_sNs200_edt_grt_trans_classhvm_list",
				"TestNames": [
				"SCN_ATOM_SF::TRANS_4C_ATOM_VMIN_K_CHKATOMF3_2000_GRT0_1005"
				]
			}
			]
	},
	{
		"Name":"CCPUP1A1APLIST",
			"Information": [
			{
				"Patlist":"scn_grt_x_vccia_f3_chkf3_sNs200_edt_grt_trans_classhvm_list",
				"TestNames": [
				"SCN_ATOM_SF::TRANS_4C_ATOM_VMIN_K_CHKATOMF3_2000_GRT0_1005",
				]
			}
			]
	},
	{
		"Name":"CHPUP1A1APLIST",
			"Information": [
			{
				"Patlist":"fun_atom_bilbo_chk_f1_list",
				"TestNames": [
				"FUN_ATOM_CADL::BILBO_8C_ATOM_VMIN_K_CHKATOMF1_0800_X_X"
				]
			},
			{
				"Patlist":"func_sa_tam_chk_f2_list",
				"TestNames": [
				"FUN_SA_CADL::TAM_8C_SAPF0_VMIN_K_CHKSAF2_1067_X_X",
				"FUN_SA_CADL::TAM_8C_SAPF0_VMIN_K_VMAXSAFX_1067_EMAX_X"
				]
			}
			]
	}
	],
	"ProcessTypes": [
		{
			"Name":"CLASSHOT",
			"StepName":"CLASSHOT",
			"UnitInfo":[
				{
				"VId":"D0RT545100097" ,
				"EFuse":"D030808E_473_-02_+04" ,
				"ConfigDetails":[
					{
					"ConfigSetName":"CHPUP1A1BPLIST" ,
					"ExecutionMode":"SHORT" 
					},
					{
					"ConfigSetName":"CHPUP1A1APLIST" ,
					"ExecutionMode":"SHORT" 
					}
				]
				},
				{
				"VId":"D0RT545100244" ,
				"EFuse":"D030808E_420_-04_-09" ,
				"ConfigDetails":[
					{
					"ConfigSetName":"CHPUP1A1BPLIST" ,
					"ExecutionMode":"SHORT" 
					},
					{
					"ConfigSetName":"CHPUP1A1APLIST" ,
					"ExecutionMode":"FULL" 
					}
				]
				}
		]
	}
	]
}

```

Field details:
- _**MaestroTpName**_: Optional field specifiyng the comma separated list of MAESTRO TP names which generated the PUP predictions model
- _**PatternsFilePath**_: Optional field specifiyng the patterns file path. If specified - it overrides the path defined in the PUP instance
- _**PupConfigSets**_: Field to provide a list of all the configuration settings. Every configuration setting contains the following fields: `Name` and `Information`
  - _**Name**_: Field to provide the configuration setting's name. This name value should be unique between all the configuration settings.
  - _**Information**_: Field to provide a list of configurations under the pup configuration setting's name. Every entry should contains at least the `Patlist` and `TestNames` fields, and optionally the `InFlowOptimizationInputs` field.
    - _**Patlist**_: Field to provide the patlist name. This patlist name should be unique in the whole configuration file.
    - _**TestNames**_: Field to provide a list of test instance names. This field's value is not considered by the PupService but it is required to customs processing.
    - _**InFlowOptimizationInputs**_: Optional field to provide a list of optimization conditions. Every entry of the list should be a json object, this means that every entry should be contained between bracket symbols, and has its `field name` and `value`. For instance, **`{"FieldName": "FieldValue"}`**. It's important to emphasize, there is not expecting that optimization is built with more than one field-value pair.
- _**ProcessTypes**_: Field to provide a list of all the process types with them unit information. Every process type contains the following fields: `Name`, `StepName`, and `UnitInfo`.
  - _**Name**_: Field to provide the process type's name.
  - _**StepName**_: Field to provide the process type's step name.
  - _**UnitInfo**_: Field to provide a list of the unit info. Every unit info should contain the following fields: `EFuse` and `ConfigDetails`, and optionally the `VId` field.
    - _**VId**_: Optional field to provide an expected **Visual ID** or **VId**.
    - _**EFuse**_: Field to provide an expected **EFuse**.
    - _**ConfigDetails**_: Field to provide a list of configuration details. Every configuration should contain a `ConfigSetName` and `ExecutionMode` field.
      - _**ConfigSetName**_: Field to provide a defined PUP configuration setting in the `PupConfigSets` section.
      - _**ExecutionMode**_: Field to provide the execution mode of the previous `ConfigSetName`. There are three types of execution mode as follows: **SHORT**, **FULL** and **MONITOR**. The `SHORT` option indicates that the expected patlist to execute is the **slim version** defined in the [pattern file](#Pattern-file), meanwhile, this decision is not overridden by any other input as the `InFlowOptimizationInputs` executed by a callback. The `FULL` option indicates that the expected patlist to execute is the **full version** with any change performed. The `MONITOR` option indicates that the patlist to execute is the **full version** in monitor mode.


# Creating Slim patlist version mechanism
This section will provide an overview of the implementation of the **slim patlist version** describes in the [pattern file](#Pattern-file), in order to provide a complete perspective to the user.

Because of some limitation in the previous versions of TOS37, where a deep copy is not possible, the current implementation of the slim version is performed creating a **copy** of the patlist indicated (where the new patlist name is the full patlist name plus _**_slimVersion**_) and **disabling** the occurrences of the patterns to disable.

# So to emphasize , _**PUP IS ONLY SUPPORTED on TOS37 and up TOS versions**_.

For instance, let's have the below patlist definition:
```
GlobalPList example_plist [PreBurst pre_burst_example_plist] {
   Pat A;
   Pat B;
   Pat C;
   Pat B;
   Pat D;
   Pat B;
   Pat E;
   Pat F;
}
```

And the slim version definition as follows in the [pattern file](#Pattern-file):
```json
{
    "PerPatlistPatternsToDisable": [
        {
            "Patlist":"example_plist",
            "PatternsToDisable": [{
                    "Pattern": "F"
                },
                {
                    "Pattern": "B",
                    "Occurrence": "2,3"
            }]
        }
    ]
}
```

So, the slim patlist resulted in the TOS memory is equivalent to the below patlist definition:
```
GlobalPList example_plist_slimVersion [PreBurst pre_burst_example_plist] {
   Pat A;
   Pat B;
   Pat C;
   Pat D;
   Pat E;
}
```

# Triggering the slim patlist
This section will cover conditions and settings in order to execute the **slim patlist version** defined in the [pattern file](#Pattern-file). The explanation will assume that both of the configuration files are well populated and available in the flow of the Test Program.

In order to execute. the **slim patlist** there are two steps to perform. The first of them is the _VId_ and _EFuse_ matching, in order to get a defined pup configuration setting. And the second step is to verify if the obtained configuration contains the current instance's patlist, if this condition is achieved the **slinm patlist** will be used.

## VId and EFuse matching
This step is perform for every `UnitInfo` in the `ProcessTypes` section in the [pup file](#PUP-file). The goal is to match the VId (if it is defined) and the EFuse's values defined in the configuration file with the current ones. The VId value is obtained reading the UserVar **SCVars.SC_VISUALID**, while the EFuse value is obtained with DFF Service's help.

This step process is illustrated as follows:

::: mermaid
graph TD
  Start(Start) --> VidCondition{Is Vid available?}
  VidCondition --> |Yes| VidComparison[Compare Vid]
  VidCondition --> |No| EFuseComparison[Compare EFuse]
  VidComparison --> EFuseComparison
  EFuseComparison --> MatchesCondition{Matches Condition?}
  MatchesCondition --> |Yes| PerformMatchAction[Verify for a valid configuration setting]
  PerformMatchAction --> End
  MatchesCondition --> |No| End(End)
:::

If the matching occurs, the `ConfigSetName` and `ExecutionMode` values are taken and considered for the [next step](#Verify-the-current-patlist).

## Verify the current patlist
This step will verify the ConfigSetName's value obtained from the [previous step](#VId-and-EFuse-matching), and verify if its definition in the `PupConfigSets` section contains an `Information` with the current instance's patlist.

If the definition of the ConfigSetName's value contains the current patlist, what will happen depends on the ExecutionMode's value.

# Patlist execution
The plist executions depends on the result of the steps explained in the [previous section](#Triggering-the-slim-patlist). If the previous steps do not match the conditions, the patlist to be executed is the **full patlist version**. But if the conditions are met: VId and EFuse matching and the pup configuration setting considers the current patlist; the **ExecutionMode**'s value will directly define the patlist to execute. If the value is **SHORT** the **slim version** will be executed, otherwise the **full version**.

# How to enable PUP on TP

*Init flow*

Add instance which registers C# callback in the user code maintained by the TP.
This user code is responsible for checking InFlow optimizations conditions (see above)

For example if the PUP user code dll called PupCallBacks

[PupCallBacks.cs](./.attachments/PupCallBacks-cc87ad15-b9af-4faf-8b50-27697270b181.cs)

Here is the documentation for that user code

[PupCallBacksDoc.docx](./.attachments/IsPlistEligibleForTTR%20Doc-c89f0378-c316-40d3-a2ae-0b2d5de74569.docx)

```
Test PupCallBacks RegisterPupInflowOptionizationsCallbacks
{
	LogLevel = "PRIME_DEBUG";
}
```

::: mermaid
 graph LR;
 A[RegisterPupInflowOptionizationsCallbacks]
:::

*Main flow*

PUP needs to know the current die ULT to be able to match the one specified in the PUP json file.
Currently the way to pass the ULT to Prime is this - > FuseRead is executed and raw capture data is exported to uservar FUSEUFGL.S_PKG_FULLBINARY . 
After that instance need to add Prime's ULTDecoder instance which decodes the ULT from the above user var and saves it into Prime's DB. 
```
Test PrimeUltDecoderTestMethod PRIME_ULT_DECODER
{
	CtvOrder = "Msb";
	DieIdNames = "U1"; #aliases decoded ULT with die Id "U1"
	ExpectedBitCount = 56;
	UserVarsList = "FUSEUFGL.S_PKG_FULLBINARY";
}
```
Then it is needed to indicate what is the current die we are working on (theoretically it can be multi die environment with U1,U2,U3,U4 .... dies)

This can be done done by calling Evergreen's user function or Prime's user code
```
Test iCUserFuncTest SetCurrentDieId
{
	function_name = "DFF_UF!SetCurrentDieId";
	function_parameter = "U1";
}
```

In the end flow need to add an instance of Prime's PupTestMethod.
It has all the PUP related knobs which are initialized during INIT .
During the MAIN flow it prints the per unit PUP report described in PUP test method wiki

```
Test PrimePUPTestMethod PrintItuff_PUP
{
	Mode = "Enabled";
	LogLevel = "PRIME_DEBUG";
	PupDebugMode = "Enabled";
}
```

::: mermaid
 graph LR;
 A[Start] --> B[...]
B --> C[FuseRead]
C --> |Populates ULT for Prime| D[Prime's UltDecoder]
D --> |sets current die id to U1| E[SetCurrentDieId]
E --> F[...]
F --> |EndFlow| G[PUPTestMethod]
:::

# Ituff ouput
## Lot level output

During lot start a PUP summary printed to ituff level 4 - data printed is relevant for current process step and type

```
4_tsattrs_PUP_MODE,ENABLED
4_tsattrs_PUP_PATTERNS_DIR,C:\development\prime\validation\regressionTps\hdmt\Modules\PUP\PUP\InputFiles\BOM\1002\patterns.pup.json
4_tsattrs_PATTERNS_FILE_VERSION,1234
4_tsattrs_PUP_FILE_PATH,~HDMT_TPL_DIR\Modules\PUP\PUP\InputFiles\testingLot_2041.pup.json
4_tsattrs_MSTP_NAME,ADL0000D00S135,ADL0000E00S137
4_tsattrs_P:FastShortBase,search_plist_2|search_plist_4|search_plist_5
4_tsattrs_P:FastFullBase,search_plist3
4_tsattrs_P:FastCacheBase2,pup_cached_plist
4_tsattrs_I:FastCacheBase,PUP::FASTExecutePupSlimPlistShortModeResolvedByCache_F0^PUP::FASTExecuteFullPlistExcludeCriteriaBaseNumber_P1
4_tsattrs_I:PrimeOnlySearchFull,PUP::PrimeVminSearchFull_F0
4_tsattrs_I:PrimeOnlySearchShort,PUP::PrimeVminSearchShort_F0
4_tsattrs_I:NoMatchingFastShortBase,PUP::FASTExecuteFullPlistNoMatchingCase_P1
```
- _**PUP_MODE**_: indicates the global PUP enabling mode - according to the "Mode" parameter in PrimePupTestMethod
- _**PUP_PATTERNS_DIR**_: indicates the path of the PTD (patterns to disable) file which was processed
- _**PATTERNS_FILE_VERSION**_: indicates the version of the PTD (patterns to disable) file which was processed (appears as "Version" field in the file)
- _**PUP_FILE_PATH**_: indicates the path of the PUP file generated by the iBLL which was processed
- _**MSTP_NAME**_: indicates the MAESTRO TP names which are mentioned in the PUP file
- _**P:**_: prints (comma separated) per configset targeted plist names (pipe separated)
- _**I:**_: prints (comma separated) per configset targeted instance names (pipe separated)

## Unit level output

For each unit PrimePupTestMethod which must be location at the End flow prints the PUP summary in the below format

```
2_tname_pup_configsets
2_strgval_FastShortBase,FastCacheBase2,FastMonitorBase,FastMonitorValidation,FastCacheBase
2_tname_pup_mode
2_strgval_S,S,M,M,S
2_tname_pup_count
2_strgval_3|0|0,0,1,1,0
2_tname_pup_excluderesult
2_strgval_F|F|F,F,NA,NA,F
2_tname_pup_excludereasoncount
2_strgval_0|0|0,2,3,0,0
```

- _**pup_configsets**_: comma separated list of active configsets
- _**pup_mode**_: for each configset indicated in the pup_configsets token specifies the execution mode for the that configset - "S" for short , "M" for monitor , "F" for full. the order of the execution modes are alighned according to the order of the configsets
- _**pup_count**_: indicates the number of occurrences when the specified in pup_configsets token configset was active during the main flow. The number can be higher than 1  , since configset can consist of number of plists & instances 
- _**pup_excluderesult**_: If the plist was asked to be executed in short mode and did it that way for all its occurrences then no exclusion happened resulting in excluderesult is false "F". If not, exclude result is true "T". for monitor, this doesnt apply - "NA".
						   pipe separates between configsets and comma separates between plists in configset
- _**pup_excludereasoncount**_: indicates the per plist number if times where exclude criteria was applied -  i.e. reverting the model decision of short prediction to full based on the current unit flow metrics
						   pipe separates between configsets and comma separates between plists in configset



### PUP implementation on SORT / SDS                           

On SORT / SDS sockets there is no InfraBLL which could not generate pup.json file.
Thus differrent approach is required on those sockets - which will be described below

Supporting PUP on SDS required SORT automation (DPC) upgrade to new tool called NAGA 

SCVars collection UserVars required to be defined to support the NAGA sort automation:

```
String SC_RESERVED1 = ""; # contains the per plist predictions populated by DPC automation. The predictions provided by tulip script
String SC_RESERVED2 = ""; # Used to indicate to PUP if for currently running die need to print TT data or not
String SC_RESERVED3 = ""; # Reserved user var for PUP SDS logic - currently unused
String SC_RESERVED4 = ""; # Used in SORT to indicate to PUP if currently running die is already tested
```

The main PUP process shift here is that unlike in class where the model is produced during the MAESTRO socket and InfraBLL generates the pup.json file based on it -  in SORT the situation is differrent.
MAESTRO socket is located after SORT socket and InfraBLL is not operational in SORT.
That's why in SORT the model is executed inline during the main flow in the scope of the execution of a targeted by a PUP model plist.
The result of model execution is the execution mode for that plist  -  short , monitor or full.

The execution mode for plist targeted by NPR is generated by the NAGA automation per each unit.


The below chart illustrates the PUP SDS logic stages in INIT and MAIN flow:

![image.png](./.attachments/pup_sds.PNG).


Here below each stage will be explained

- _**Init Flow**_:
  - _**Clear PUP Uservars**_: During INIT flow Prime clears the PUP SDS uservars by setting the value "CLEARED" in those uservars

        ```
        String SC_RESERVED1 = "CLEARED"; 
        String SC_RESERVED2 = "CLEARED"; 
        String SC_RESERVED3 = "CLEARED"; 
        String SC_RESERVED4 = "CLEARED"; 
        ```
   - _**Process NPR targeted plists**_: NPR targeted plists are provided in the optional parameter of PUP instance called TargetedPlistsFilePath. It provides the path to the file in csv format where all the targeted plists (by NPR and PUP are specified).

        ```
        plist alias	plist name   	skip percentage	 monitor percentage (out of the skip %)
        P1	        search_plist_4	10	                20
        P22	        search_plist	10	                20
        P23	        search_plist_1	10	                20
        P12	        search_plist_2	10	                20
        P13	        search_plist_5	10	                20

        ```

        This file is read both by Prime and the NAGA automation tool (similar to CLASS's station controller - the Skyline)

        Prime reads only first 2 columns - plistAlias and plistName- plist alias is a plist short identifier is used for ituff printout in order to save the ituff and uservar space (SC_RESERVED1/2/3/4 uservars support up to 1000 characters - NAGA limitation).
        NAGA reads the skip percentage and monitor percentage columns -  they are useed in order to calculate the skip percentage (the percentage of short execution modes in the lot). The monitor rate is used by NAGA tool to calculate the rate of the monitor execution modes.
        
        By default all the plists specified there are targeted by NPR. If some of those plists are targeted by PUP model too - the model targeting takes the precedence over NPR

   - _**Process PUP targeted plists**_: PUP targeted plists are provided by the IASG user code defines the PUP targeted plists along with model files (ONNX format) and their input. Those plists are subset of the NPR targeted plists and have a precedence over NPR

- _**LotStart Flow**_:
  - _**Print PUP Header to Ituff **_: During lot start flow PUP header is printed to ituff in the below format

        ```
        4_tsattrs_PUP_MODE,ENABLED
        4_tsattrs_PUP_PATTERNS_DIR,C:\temp\BOM\1002\patterns.pup.json
        4_tsattrs_PATTERNS_FILE_VERSION,1234
        4_tsattrs_PUP_FILE_PATH,UNAVAILABLE
        4_tsattrs_MSTP_NAME,UNAVAILABLE
        4_tsattrs_MODEL_PLISTS_MAPPING_P22,search_plist
        4_tsattrs_MODEL_PLISTS_MAPPING_P23,search_plist_1
        4_tsattrs_MODEL_PLISTS_MAPPING_P12,search_plist_2
        4_tsattrs_MODEL_PLISTS_MAPPING_P13,search_plist_5
        4_tsattrs_NPR_PLISTS_MAPPING_P1,search_plist_4
        4_tsattrs_NPR_PLISTS_MAPPING_P22,search_plist
        4_tsattrs_NPR_PLISTS_MAPPING_P23,search_plist_1
        4_tsattrs_NPR_PLISTS_MAPPING_P12,search_plist_2
        4_tsattrs_NPR_PLISTS_MAPPING_P13,search_plist_5
        4_tsattrs_PLISTS_NOT_FOUND_P33,not_existing_plist
        4_tsattrs_MODEL_VERSIONS,Model1=111|Model2=222|Model3=333|Model4=444
        ```

      - PUP_FILE_PATH must not be provided (otherwise Prime fails INIT)
      - MSTP_NAME is NA in SORT
      - All the plists targeted by NPR (provided in targeted plists file) are printed here with **NPR_PLISTS_MAPPING_<PlistAlias>** token
      - All the plists targeted by MODEL (provided in IASG user code) are printed here with **MODEL_PLISTS_MAPPING_<PlistAlias>** token
      - Plists specified in targeted plists file but not loaded in the TP are printed here with **PLIST_NOT_FOUND_<PlistAlias>** token
      - If PUP model is enabled (does not have to be  - NPR only usage is also valid) -  there is a version number(s) for the model. It is printed here with  **MODEL_VERSIONS** token

- _**MAIN Flow**_:
  - _**Automation**_: 
   - Before the unit is executed the sort automation tool (NAGA) populates the per plist execution modes and indication whether or not to print unit test time to ituff. 
     - The execution modes are populated based on the skip rate and monitor rate in the targeted plist file and other factors.
     - Print test time indicator is also populated based on sample rate provided probably in the recipe

   - NAGA checks if the unit is executed for the first time in the job or is being retested.
     - If the unit is being retested the automation populates the previously executed per plist execution modes and indicator whether or not to print the test time for this die.
   - The execution modes are set to SC_RESERVED1 uservar in format of: **<PlistAlias1>=<ExecutionMode1>|<PlistAlias2>=<ExecutionMode2>**...
   - The execution modes are S - short , M - monitor , F - full
   - The test time print indication is specified as "Y" or "N"

        Example:
        ```
        SC_RESERVED1 = "P1=S|P22=M|P23=F|P12=S|P13=M"
        SC_RESERVED2 = "Y"
        SC_RESERVED4 = "N"
        ```

  - _**MAIN flow execution**_:
    - _**Prime**_:  
     - At the beginning of the main flow execution Prime decodes the execution modes populated by NAGA in SC_RESERVED1 uservar and saved it for further handling.
     - During main flow execution when targeted plist is executed Prime executes the plist version defined by execution of for this plist - short , monitor or full.
     - If the plist is targeted by PUP model and not only by NPR - Prime executes the model during targted instance execution in order to resolve by inline inference the execution mode for its plist
     - **To summarise**: NPR execution mode is known at the beginning of the main flow and PUP execution mode is resolved during plist execution by running model inference

  - _**End Flow**_:
    - PUP Test method instance executed during unit end flow prints the unit execution summary.
    - Example:
        ```
        2_tname_pup_configsets
        2_strgval_NA
        2_tname_pup_mode
        2_strgval_NA
        2_tname_pup_count
        2_strgval_NA
        2_tname_pup_excluderesult
        2_strgval_NA
        2_tname_pup_excludereasoncount
        2_strgval_NA
        2_tname_pup_npr_execution_modes
        2_strgval_P1=s|P22=M|P23=F|P12=S|P13=M
        2_tname_pup_dieinfo1
        2_strgval_P1=S|P22=M|P23=F|P12=S|P13=M
        2_tname_pup_dieinfo2
        2_strgval_N
        2_tname_pup_dieinfo3
        2_strgval_NA
        2_tname_pup_dietested
        2_strgval_Y
        ```
    - All the tokens relevant for class (pup_configsets , pup_mode , pup_count, pup_excluderesult , pup_excludereasoncount) are not relevant in SORT - that's why their value is "NA"
    - **pup_npr_execution_modes** -  specifies the original NPR based per targeted plist execution modes populated by the automation at thye beginning of the MAIN flow. basically it is a reflection is **SC_RESERVED1** uservar
    - **pup_dieinfo1** -  specifies the actual per targeted plist execution modes. The execution modes might be differrent in case some plists are targeted also by the model and its inference for plist execution mode occurs later in the main flow when targted instance is executed.
        - This token name should not be changed as the NAGA automation is hooked on it in order to store each unit's plists execution modes in order to restore them in case of retest.
    - **pup_dieinfo2** -  specifies the print test time indicator  - "Y" or "N". It basically reflects the **SC_RESERVED2** uservar content.
        - This token name should not be changed as the NAGA automation is hooked on it in order to store each unit's print test time indicator in order to restore them in case of retest.        
    - **pup_dieinfo3** - not in use for now
    - **pup_dietested** - indicates that current die is executed    
        - This token name should not be changed as the NAGA automation is hooked on it to track the already tested units for the retest.
